blocks.content: Julian sat in the dark of the study next door.
He had a legal pad on the table in front of him and a pen and the coffee that had gone cold an hour ago.
He wrote down what he knew. Not what he suspected — what he knew. There was a difference and he was careful about the difference.
A group of six people. A bonfire Friday night. One person watching another with a quality of attention that was not warmth.
The one being watched: tall, at the edge of the group, fire to his right. Consistent with the man found dead tonight.
Open window in a forty-seven degree night. A man who locked his own door, lay down to rest, and didn't get up. A body running warm in its final hours.
A phone call — heated, somewhere between nine and ten. With who.
He looked at the list.
Not enough. Not yet. But enough to know it's something.
His phone buzzed on the table. He looked at the screen.
Mara.

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: Are those sirens I'm hearing about your beach? Please tell me you just watched someone get a speeding ticket.
He almost smiled.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Someone died next door. I spoke with the inspector twenty minutes ago.
Three seconds. Then:

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: Julian. You are supposed to be on vacation.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I am on vacation.

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: You spoke with the inspector.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: He came to me. Technically.

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: So much for a vacation.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Fun can come after. There's a case that needs attention.
A long pause.

blocks.speaker: Mara
blocks.content: Be careful. And sleep at some point. You're useless to anyone when you don't sleep.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I'll sleep when I know what I'm looking at.
She hung up. He set the phone down. Turned the legal pad to a clean page.
Wrote a name at the top. Then a question mark beside it. Then five more names underneath.
Because in a room like this, everyone is a question mark until they aren't.
